findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:175, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:183, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:195, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:353, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:375, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:383 — the trunk-sort exchange and the nursing-letter exchange each run 10+ consecutive paragraphs on dialogue/action without an explicit Tiana thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment, so the chapter briefly reads like staged coverage rather than close-third fiction.
findings.problem: Two major dialogue runs go exterior-only for too long. That violates the brief's interiority requirement and weakens the chapter's strongest asset: Tiana's close, caustic consciousness under pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Re-focalize the run beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:175 and the run beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:353. Add concise Tiana interior beats after Micah wakes, after Kerene's evidence joke lands, and after Marcia deflects the nursing-school question so the reader feels what each exchange costs in Tiana's body and self-story, not just what is said aloud.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised material around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:175 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:353 contains at least two explicit Tiana interior beats in each run (thought, bodily sensation, memory, or self-assessment) and no stretch in either exchange exceeds 9 consecutive paragraphs without such an interior beat; FAIL otherwise.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:11, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:13, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:63, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:145, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:249, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:305, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:331, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:425 — the chapter leans repeatedly on 'as if' comparison scaffolding to explain reactions and objects.
findings.problem: The repeated 'as if' template becomes a visible prose habit. By the back half of the chapter it starts to feel like default explanatory wiring rather than a chosen comparison, which slightly flattens otherwise sharp sentences.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the comparison beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:63, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:145, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:249, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:305, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:331, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:425. Replace at least four of those 'as if' constructions with direct sensory description, named judgment, or action so the line lands without comparative scaffolding.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, by reading the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:63, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:145, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:249, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:305, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:331, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:425, no more than two 'as if' explanatory comparisons remain and each replacement still conveys a concrete visual, tactile, or psychological fact; FAIL otherwise.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:53, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:295, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:375 — 'Almost.', 'Relief, already trying to stand up in him.', and 'Not long. Long enough.' rely on clipped narration for emphasis.
findings.problem: Those beats violate the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration. Repeated fragments like this read as shorthand emphasis rather than earned compression, especially in a chapter whose baseline syntax is otherwise natural and controlled.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the narration at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:53, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:295, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:375 into grammatically complete sentences that preserve the same pressure. Keep the beat sharp, but make the emphasis come from diction and pacing rather than omitted verbs or sentence fragments.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised narration at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:53, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:295, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:375 remains tense while every sentence has an explicit grammatical subject and finite verb; FAIL if any of those emphasis beats still depend on clipped fragments.

summary: Strong chapter architecture and continuity control: the document hunt stays concrete, the trunk works as archive rather than magic fix, the nursing-school packet and remittance receipts materially complicate both parents, and the dialogue remains distinct, contracted, and character-true. No continuity contradiction, sanitization drift, intimacy fade-to-black, or style-bible example-line reuse is evidenced in the allowed inputs. The fail is craft/prose-level rather than causal: long exterior-only dialogue runs thin the close-third interiority, the repeated 'as if' scaffolding becomes visible, and a few narration fragments break the constitution's anti-clipping rule, which keeps the chapter below pass-level Booker/craft/prose quality.
